expectations.text: Flags POST /getReviewComments as a verb-in-URL / RPC-style endpoint instead of a noun resource
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified the verb URL and prescribed GET /workspaces/:workspaceId/review-comments.

expectations.text: Flags the blank-body validation failure returning 200 {ok:false, reason} instead of a 4xx (400/422)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials cited this as the skill's canonical 'success masking failure' anti-pattern and prescribed 422 with the error envelope.

expectations.text: Flags the catch block returning (err as Error).stack in the 500 response body as leaking internals
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the stack-trace leak explicitly and cited the skill's 'never leak internals in a 5xx' rule.

expectations.text: Flags the PUT .../increment-vote endpoint as violating PUT's idempotency contract
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified the counter-increment-under-PUT violation and proposed POST + idempotency key or a per-voter idempotent PUT.

expectations.text: Flags the client hook parsing the response with a raw 'as ReviewCommentRecord[]' cast instead of validating it
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials cited the skill's parse-don't-cast rule and recommended a shared Zod schema with .parse().

expectations.text: Flags GET /exports for offset pagination with no next-cursor/pagination envelope
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the bare-array response and offset paging, citing the cursor/keyset preference and envelope requirement.

expectations.text: Flags GET /exports/:exportId for not setting Cache-Control/ETag despite the comment claiming it's cacheable
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials noticed the comment/implementation mismatch and proposed conditional Cache-Control based on terminal job status.

expectations.text: Flags the 404 branch returning a bare string as inconsistent with the module's own envelope error shape
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials treated this as a blocking issue and several traced the concrete client-side symptom (falls back to 'unknown_error').

expectations.text: Flags POST /exports (retryable creation) for accepting no Idempotency-Key header
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the missing idempotency key on the job-creation POST and referenced the Stripe-style cache-and-replay pattern.

expectations.text: Flags useExportStatus.ts's setTimeout(tick, 750) as busy-polling instead of server push (SSE)
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the polling loop and referenced DevDigest's own SSE precedent (useRunEvents).

expectations.text: (precision check) Does not flag api-client.ts as a violation
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly called out api-client.ts as already correct (schema.parse, ApiError normalization, 204 handling) and said not to churn it.

expectations.text: (precision check) Does not flag the DELETE handler or digestExportsErrorHandler as violations
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials placed both in an explicit 'already correctly designed' section; the redundant if/else branches in DELETE were noted only as a style nit, never as a contract violation.

expectations.text: Flags the lack of a distinguishable terminal SSE event (done/error) to signal stream completion vs failure
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials identified this as the central bug, explaining that success and failure both just close the socket and that EventSource will auto-reconnect either way.

expectations.text: Flags the missing per-event 'id:' field needed for Last-Event-ID-based resume on reconnect
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials flagged the absence of SSE ids and Last-Event-ID handling and connected it to DevDigest's stated replay-buffer-first pattern.

expectations.text: Flags useRunEvents.ts for having no onerror handler / no observable connection-health signal
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials pointed out onmessage-only wiring and recommended surfacing connection/error state to the UI.

expectations.text: (precision check) Does not flag the choice of SSE itself or the push-not-poll design as a violation
expectations.diagnosis_reason: All 5 trials explicitly praised the SSE/push choice in a 'what's already correct' section and did not recommend reverting to polling.

fixture_issues: Minor, non-blocking: in eval 1 trial 1, the model labeled the Cache-Control/ETag finding (F2) as '(Out of scope for this skill)' while still correctly citing this skill's own rule 9 and fixing it per the skill's guidance -- a labeling quirk in that single trial's self-organization, not a missed expectation or a skill/fixture defect.
